authoritative_sources.title: 45 CFR Part 98 - Child Care and Development Fund
authoritative_sources.publisher: Electronic Code of Federal Regulations / U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal regulation for child-care subsidy and health-and-safety administration. It covers provider monitoring, substantiated parental complaints, background checks, and error-rate record retention, all of which directly constrain child-care documentation work.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: program-governance intake and official-record boundary setting
used_for: public complaint and monitoring record expectations

authoritative_sources.title: Monitoring and Inspections
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official child-care guidance for inspections, monitoring, and substantiated complaint visibility. It grounds which inspection and complaint artifacts belong in the lane and which public-record questions require governance handling.

authoritative_sources.title: Health and Safety Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official child-care health-and-safety baseline naming the categories of documents and controls programs must maintain, including immunization, medication, emergency planning, and building safety.

authoritative_sources.title: National Database of Child Care Licensing Regulations
authoritative_sources.publisher: Child Care Technical Assistance Network / Administration for Children and Families
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official federal technical-assistance reference for locating state licensing regulations and agency contacts. It is authoritative for narrowing public federal baselines to the actual jurisdiction-specific record, retention, incident, and inspection rules that govern the lane.

authoritative_sources.title: Caring for Our Children
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Resource Center for Health and Safety in Child Care and Early Education
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized national early-care-and-education health and safety standards source that informs evidence expectations for child health, medication, incident, and environment documentation without overriding licensing law.

authoritative_sources.title: CACFP State Agency
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Agriculture Food and Nutrition Service
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official USDA guidance confirming state monitoring and recordkeeping obligations for CACFP participants. It constrains meal-count, reimbursement, and integrity records when a child-care program participates in CACFP.

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and jurisdiction classification
workflow_stages.description: Identify provider type, jurisdiction, licensing status, subsidy participation, and whether the request is in scope for child-care documentation rather than licensing, privacy, or approval authority.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Child, staff, and program record validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate child enrollment files, attendance, health and safety records, staff background-check and training records, and inspection or complaint artifacts against the governing public baseline and tenant policy.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Administrative maintenance and evidence packaging
workflow_stages.description: Prepare completeness summaries, status notes, record update notes, and evidence packets for licensing, incident, subsidy, or internal follow-up without deciding the underlying outcome.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Retention, access, and escalation handling
workflow_stages.description: Escalate custody, disclosure, retention, record-conflict, and official-record authority issues instead of overresolving them inside an administrative lane.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: Child enrollment packets and annual update forms
Emergency contact, release authorization, and authorized-pickup records
Immunization, medication, and child health documentation
Attendance and sign-in or sign-out records
Incident, injury, and medication administration reports
Staff background-check status and required training logs
Inspection reports, substantiated complaint records, and corrective-action evidence
CCDF subsidy attendance or authorization records when applicable
CACFP meal-count and claim-support records when applicable
systems_of_record: Child care management or student information platform
State licensing portal or licensing document repository
Subsidy or CCDF attendance and claims system
Document management repository for child, staff, and licensing files
Audit logging and retention control systems

allowed: Classify scope, validate completeness, and prepare child-care documentation summaries, update notes, and evidence packets.
Identify missing or conflicting child-care records and route the request to the correct owner or adjacent lane.
Apply stricter tenant controls when they narrow but do not contradict the public baseline.
escalate: Jurisdiction, provider type, or subsidy or CACFP participation is unknown and affects the governing record set.
Retention, access, custody, or public-record questions require legal or privacy-owner interpretation.
Inspection, complaint, attendance, or incident records conflict across systems and source precedence is unresolved.
refuse: Requests to backdate, delete, or sanitize official records.
Requests to approve licensing closure, subsidy eligibility, child exclusion, or other official determinations outside documentation scope.
Requests that need legal, clinical, licensed, safety-critical, or policy-override authority.

domain_failure_modes: Treating child-care documentation as generic admin work and omitting child health, pickup authorization, or incident records.
Certifying completeness without confirming which licensing jurisdiction and provider type govern the file.
Using attendance maintenance authority to alter subsidy or reimbursement outcomes.
Disclosing child or incident records without resolving custody, privacy, or authorized-access flags.
Treating public inspection or complaint records as interchangeable with confidential child records.
Ignoring staff background-check and training evidence when the request depends on licensing-safe documentation.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Required record or evidence package is incomplete
Retention, privacy, or access rule conflicts with the request
Stakeholder asks the lane to approve an official record decision it cannot own
Low-confidence escalation when required facts or authority are missing

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State-specific retention intervals and inspection response windows
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Exact retention periods, parent access rights, and incident-reporting timelines vary materially by jurisdiction and provider type.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require jurisdiction lookup through the licensing database or tenant policy before concluding retention or disclosure readiness.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Program participation in CCDF subsidy and CACFP
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Attendance, reimbursement, and meal-count record requirements differ when the tenant does not participate in these programs.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Treat subsidy and CACFP participation as required tenant facts and block program-specific record claims when participation is unknown.

unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Who may access a child record can depend on local custody orders, tenant policy, and state privacy or licensing rules.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Escalate access questions to privacy or legal owners when custody documentation is incomplete or conflicting.
